repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id_v1 only retained an open minimal-order handle; it did not state a full-truncation 2m+2 direction-recovery theorem.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorems 4.4–4.7, gives a k=m+2 same-order cumulant-matrix rank/determinant rule, not recovery from the complete truncation through 2m+2.
ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 gives a one-latent higher-cumulant asymmetry criterion, not an arbitrary-m opposite-arrow overlap ideal or codimension theorem.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id_v1 warns that failure of one witness is not a global information lower bound, but supplies no opposite-arrow twin family.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction supplies a sufficient order-(2m+3) matrix criterion, not a generic reverse-twin lower construction through 2m+1.
ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorem 4.2, already gives the m=0 third-cumulant rank diagnostic.
SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 uses higher-order-cumulant rank conditions for recursive discovery, not a semialgebraic real atlas of a bivariate opposite-arrow overlap.

novelty_flags.label: Published survey for the flagship minimal-order kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: Add CaiHara2026, which gives finite-sample higher-cumulant recovery of a sparsest LvLiNGAM DAG with no restriction on the number of latent confounders; it does not settle the finite-order frontier, but is a closer omitted comparator.

novelty_flags.label: HoyerShimizuKerminen2008
novelty_flags.one_line: The bibliography calls this a 2008 UAI paper by three authors, but the three-author finite-compatible-set work is the 2006 preprint, while the 2008 IJAR article has the title 'Estimation of causal effects using linear non-Gaussian causal models with hidden variables' and adds Markus Palviainen.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 3: lower-order twins
novelty_flags.one_line: Equality of complex Zariski closures commits no nondegenerate real independent-source twin family; give a moment-feasible generic construction or name an explicit construction route and downgrade the real-law converse to an OEQ.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 and definition: recovery map
soundness_flags.one_line: R_m is defined by solving A_m, but A_m simultaneously requires forward and reverse representations, so it has no solution at t outside E_m—the very locus where Theorem 1 invokes R_m.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2 and Theorem 3: complex closures versus real laws
soundness_flags.one_line: C^→ and C^← are complex Zariski closures, whereas the conclusions assert actual real independent-source representations; closure intersection and elimination do not by themselves produce finite-moment real source laws or real reverse twins.

soundness_flags.label: Proposition 1: unconfounded reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: For m=0 the declared K equals 2 and R_0 uses only T_2, while the claimed Chen reduction needs the third-order matrix; no appended-order recovery map is defined, so the stated reduction does not follow.

soundness_flags.label: ass:forward-axis-model and ass:reverse-axis-model
soundness_flags.one_line: Both are tagged as standard bivariate linear non-Gaussian models, but their conditions require only independence, centering, and finite moments; they permit Gaussian sources and vanishing informative cumulants.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: honest_scope: population-only claim
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: No uniform finite-sample inference, dependent-latent extension, or direct-effect coefficient identification is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any repaired headline remains a population direction-identification result conditional on the corrected LvLiNGAM source assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace R_m by separate forward and reverse simultaneous-Waring decomposition maps, then state an explicit selection predicate based on the recovered coordinate-axis direction.
Construct real non-Gaussian source families for m=1 and m=2 with explicit cumulants through K, then state the generic deformation route that yields real lower-order reverse twins.
Define a separate m=0 T_3 recovery map and prove its matrix entries and rank comparison exactly match ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction Theorem 4.2.
Add CaiHara2026 and correct the Hoyer citation; distinguish their full-DAG or finite-compatible-model goals from the proposed finite-cumulant information-order frontier.

recommended_next_step: Revise the recovery construction, real-law feasibility route, m=0 reduction, source assumptions, and literature map before advancing to Stage 0.
